thread.title_full: Himachal Pradesh Government to Introduce College Ranking System for Quality Education | Shimla News - Times of India

author: TNN
title: Himachal Pradesh Government to Introduce College Ranking System for Quality Education | Shimla News - Times of India
text: Shimla: The Himachal Pradesh govt is considering ranking its 138 colleges and a formula for this is being prepared, chief minister Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu said on Saturday.Presiding over a programme of the alumni association at the Center of Excellence Govt Degree College, Sanjauli, Sukhu said the state govt wanted to strengthen the facilities in the colleges and all other govt educational institutions.The state govt is emphasising on providing quality education to the students of the Himachal and there is no dearth of funds for this work, he said.The CM, meanwhile, said the arts block, girls’ hostel and parking at the Sanjauli college complex would be built in two years, adding that he would come to lay the foundation stones for these works. Sukhu also announced Rs 50 lakh for the alumni association. Many changes will be witnessed in the next budget where special emphasis will be on the field of education, he assured.Recalling the old days, the CM said: “I will always remember the time spent in this college. My media advisor Naresh Chauhan contested elections against me in college, but I won. All student organisations used to go on strike for their demands, but everyone’s thinking was to strengthen the institution.” Other alumni of the college, Justice Sandeep Sharma of the Himachal Pradesh high court, said the institute had contributed significantly in giving a new direction to his career, for which he would always be indebted to it. tnnWe also published the following articles recentlySukhu-led Congress govt has destroyed Himachal: BJP's Jairam Thakur after court attaches Himachal BhawanFormer Himachal Pradesh Chief Minister Jairam Thakur has sharply criticized the current Congress government for alleged mismanagement. He pointed to a court order to attach Himachal Bhavan in Delhi over unpaid bills as evidence of the government's failures. Thakur expressed concern over the state's financial stability and criticized the government's handling of hydroelectric projects and agreements with central government entities.115439306CM Sukhu reacts to HC attaching Himachal Bhawan in Delhi; BJP blames govts 'financial mismanagement'The Himachal Pradesh High Court's order to attach Himachal Bhawan in Delhi over unpaid electricity bills has sparked controversy. Chief Minister Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu stated he is yet to review the order, while the BJP criticized the government's financial management. Former CM Jairam Thakur expressed concern over potential asset auctions if the situation persists.115440374State govt issues chargesheet to MGM Medical College deanMGM Medical College dean, Dr. Sanjay Dixit, faces a chargesheet over alleged corruption related to incentive disbursement. He is accused of granting incentives to doctors engaged in private practice and misappropriating funds under the Ayushman Bharat scheme. Dr. Dixit has 21 days to respond, failing which a departmental inquiry will be initiated. Dr.115499914
